summary: Completed testing of hierarchical permissions system. Backend APIs working correctly. Frontend permissions modal functional. Level 2 users can manage subordinates' permissions but are limited to permissions they have.

integration_issues.issue: Level 2 user (Eng Medhat Hussien) sees permissions modal but some permission groups show 'تحديد الكل' instead of 'إلغاء الكل' - indicating those permissions are not available to them

action_items: ENHANCEMENT REQUESTED BY USER: Admin (بيت الخبرة) should have full control over ALL permissions for ALL users they create
ENHANCEMENT REQUESTED: When Admin removes a permission from Level 2 user, it should automatically cascade and remove from Level 3 users
ENHANCEMENT REQUESTED: Level 2 users should only be able to grant permissions they themselves have to Level 3 users
CURRENT STATUS: Backend has update_subusers_permissions() function that cascades permission removals - this is working
CURRENT STATUS: Frontend hasPermission() function correctly checks user.permissions array for non-admin users
VERIFY: Test the cascade behavior when admin removes permission from Level 2 - does it auto-remove from Level 3?
critical_code_review_comments: Backend update_subusers_permissions() at line 1025 correctly implements recursive cascade
Frontend Layout.js hasPermission() at line 24-29 correctly returns true for admin, checks permissions array for others
Users.js permissions modal at line 1103 shows all permissions but Level 2 can only toggle permissions they have
context_for_next_testing_agent: Hierarchical permissions system is implemented. Admin can manage all users. Level 2 users can manage their subordinates but limited to their own permissions. The cascade logic in backend (update_subusers_permissions) removes permissions from child users when parent loses them. User requested enhancement to ensure this works dynamically across all levels.
